will be the future relations toward
the employers associations that
have antagonized organized labor
during the past few years, the &C
ton of the National Associations of
Manufacturers in prosecuting some
of the labor officials for alleged
contempt of court and violation of
an Injunction, and in raising a fund
of $1,50O,000 for the purpose of at
lacking the unions at every vulner-
able point, will have but one effect
upon the delegates, and that is to
formulate plans to present a solid
and united front to the enemy, ire
spective as to what the internal
differences may be between indi-
vidual unions and persons.


The probabilities are that steps
will be taken to accumulate a
larger defense fund or funds to be
controlled directly by the fedora
ton of the affiliated international
organizations themselves and to be
used in contests with the antagon-
istic employers associations. This
struggle on the industrial field
again leads straight into the polit-
ical arena, and for some time
feeling has been assuming shape
among the rank and file favorable
